div1.entryFree:
Hoot, 1) to cry as an owl: Mids. II, 2, 6. Caes. I, 3, 28.
2) to shout with wonder: the people fall a --ing, LLL IV, 2, 61. the rabblement --ed and clapped their chopped hands, Caes. I, 2, 245 (O. Edd. howted).
3) to shout with contempt: should be --ed at like an old tale, Wint. V, 3, 116. in --ing at Coriolanus' exile, Cor. IV, 6, 131. With an accus. denoting the effect: did h. him out o' the city, Cor. IV, 6, 123.
